summary: DeVault's April 2022 status update covers progress on Himitsu, a Plan 9-inspired secret storage system reimplemented in his new programming language (Hare) in half the code and time of the original C prototype. The language's standard library gained regex and datetime modules, with the datetime implementation including Martian time support as a demonstration of its design robustness. He hints that the language may go public soon after a few more milestones. SourceHut saw continued GraphQL improvements across multiple services, and he started a bittorrent daemon project currently blocked on HTTP/TLS support.
key_points: Himitsu, a secret storage system inspired by Plan 9's Factotum, was reimplemented in Hare in fewer than half the lines of the original C version
Hare's standard library gained regex (targeting POSIX ERE) and datetime modules, with the datetime API including Martian time support
The Hare language is approaching a public release after a few more milestones
SourceHut landed GraphQL-native webhooks for todo.sr.ht and is researching GraphQL Federation
DeVault started a bittorrent daemon in Hare, blocked on HTTP/TLS support
money_quotes: Ultimately I shelved this project for want of a better programming language to implement it with, and then I made a better programming language to implement it with.
Over the past two weeks, I have implemented something similar to where the C codebase was left, in fewer than half the lines of code and much less than half the time.
The regex implementation is simple, small, and fast, targeting POSIX ERE as a reasonably sane conservative baseline regex dialect.
As a bonus, and a little flex at how robust our design is, we've included support for Martian time.
Many readers will be happy to know that there are rumblings about possibly going public soon.
key_insight: DeVault's new language is proving its value by enabling him to reimplement projects like Himitsu in dramatically less code and time than C, while the language's standard library matures toward a public release.
